agents.cascade: The rapid increase in housing supply could drive up land/property values and taxes, leading to greater unaffordability and the risk of displacement for this demographic.

risks.title: Displacement due to Rising Housing Costs
risks.description: The rapid increase in property values and taxes as a result of the new housing policy could displace both renters and owners in multiple cities such as Toronto, Kelowna, and Vancouver. This risk is particularly acute for low-income demographics who are unable to keep up with the increased costs.
risks.affected_groups: Low-income renters and owners across major urban centers
risks.cascade_effect: The displacement could disrupt access to job opportunities and social support networks, leading to greater economic instability.

risks.title: Strain on Social Services
risks.description: As unaffordability drives people out of their homes, there is a potential surge in demand for social services aimed at housing assistance and homelessness prevention, especially in cities like Halifax and Montreal.
risks.cascade_effect: The increased demand could strain already stretched social services, exacerbating issues of housing instability.

risks.title: Increased Financial Hardship
risks.description: Higher housing and property tax costs could lead to increased financial burden on both renters and owners, reducing their ability to afford other essentials such as healthcare and groceries.
risks.affected_groups: Renters and homeowners with medium to low incomes
risks.cascade_effect: Increased financial hardship could lead to greater levels of debt and reduced economic mobility.

risks.title: Economic Inequity Amplified
risks.description: The disparity between incomes and housing costs could further divide urban populations, pushing economically disadvantaged groups to less desirable areas with limited access to opportunities.
risks.affected_groups: Low and median-income families across urban and suburban areas

risks.title: Retail and Rental Price Surge
risks.description: As home values rise, rental rates may also increase, putting additional financial pressure on tenant groups, particularly in sought-after urban neighborhoods.
risks.cascade_effect: The surge in rental prices could contribute to increased overall living costs, further straining budgets.

risk_report.blind_spots: The analysis lacks insights from indigenous and new immigrant communities who may experience unique challenges.
risk_report.key_insight: The policy's intent to increase housing stock might inadvertently lead to significant unaffordability issues in both urban and rural communities.

confidence.reason: strong StatsCan data coverage for this policy type; all agents responded successfully
confidence.caveat: This is a hypothesis generation tool. Findings should be validated against real survey data before informing policy decisions.
